item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 10 AM

All materials provided. $91 BBGS members/$130 nonmembers.

item.title: Kaleidoscope Play and Learn / Ku ciyaarista iyo barashada qalabka Kaleidoscope-ka
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Friday, March 27, 2026, 10:30am - 12pm Children listen to multilingual stories, sing songs and do simple arts and crafts with their caregivers during the group time. --------------------------------------------------------------- Carruurtu waxay dhegaystaan sheekooyinka luqadaha badan, waxay ku heesaan heeso waxayna la sameeyaan farshaxanka iyo farsamooyinka gacanta ee fudud daryeelayaashooda inta lagu jiro wakhtiga kooxda. Location Link: NewHolly Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Kaleidoscope Play & Learn offers a chance for children and their families to come play and have a good time., There is also a play time for children to explore various toys and play activities., Children learn and explore the use of art, music, toys and more. Families learn about their child&'s development and community resources available to them., Thanks to our partner East African Community Services ., ---------------------------------------------------------------, Ku ciyaarista iyo barashada qalabka Kaleidoscope-ka waxay siisaa fursad carruurta iyo qoysaskooda ay isugu yimaadaan si ay u ciyaaraan oo ayna waqti fiican ku wada qaataan. Waxa kale oo jira wakhti ay carruurtu ku sahminayaan alaabta ay caruurtu ku ciyaaraan ee kala duwan iyo hawlaha ciyaarta. Carruurtu waxay bartaan oona sahmiyaan isticmaalka farshaxanka, muusiga, alaabta ay caruurtu ku ciyaaraan iyo wax ka loo badan. Qoysasku waxay bartaan horumarka ilmahooda iyo macluumaadka/ilaha bulshada ee ay heli karaan., Waxaa mahad leh la-hawlgalaheena Adeegyada Bulshada Bariga Afrika \(East African Community Services\) . Event Logistics: This group is open to children of all abilities, ages 0 - 5 years old., Kaleidoscope Play & Learn is for Family, Friend & Neighbor Caregivers, Parents, and the children in their care. This is not a drop-off program and it is not agood fit for daycare centers, preschools, or other childcare organizations., Find out more about Early Learning & Story Time programs., ---------------------------------------------------------------, Kooxdani waxay u furan tahay carruurta qabaan dhammaan awoodaha kala duwan, da'doodu tahay 0 - 5 sano jir. Kani maaha barnaamij meel-ka-dhigis ah oo kuma habboona xarumaha xannaanada, dugsiyada barbaarinta, ama ururrada kale ee daryeelka carruurta., Wax badan ka ogow barnaamijyada Waqtiga Barashada Hore & Sheekada . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636., ---------------------------------------------------------------, Haddii aad su'aalo ka qabto dhacdadan, Na Weydii ama wac 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public., ---------------------------------------------------------------, Waxaan u sameyn karnaa wax u diyaarin dadka naafada ah ee jooga munaasabadaha ka dhacayaan Maktabadda. Fadlan la xidhiidh LEAP ugu yaraan todoba maalmood ka hor dhacdada si aad u codsato wax u sii diyaarin., Dhammaan barnaamijyada maktabaddu waa lacag la'aan oo u furan dadweynaha.,

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 11 AM

Friday, March 27, 2026, 11am - 4pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Southwest Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns. To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return., Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return., Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents willbe accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov ., You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner AARP . Event Logistics: Appointments are not required but recommended. Register here. , AARP Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025., For eligibility details, contact AARP at 888-227-7669., Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public., The Southwest Branch is accessible by the 21, 22, and C Line buses., Free parking in our lot and free street parking is available., The second-floor meeting room is accessible by elevator, or two flights of stairs with railings. ADA-accessible restrooms are available on the second floor., Ask staff if you need assistive devices \(such as magnifying glasses\) during the event.

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: BiggerPockets Value-Add Conference
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Seattle, Wa, 98121, Seattle, WA

Real estate investors looking to sharpen their execution and scale for smarter deals will not want to miss the **first-ever BiggerPockets Value-Add Conference** in Seattle on March 28, 2026. This one-day, tight-knit event has only 120 seats available and is designed specifically for experienced investors actively executing deals. Led by seasoned local investor and Million Dollar Zombie Flips host James Dainard, alongside BiggerPockets experts Dave Meyer and Henry Washington, the conference offers a rare opportunity to learn how top investors scale deals in today’s competitive real estate market through stronger underwriting, strategic renovations, optimized financing, and disciplined execution systems. Investment to attend the one-day conference starts at $500, with a limited number of spots available HERE.  The in-person event features powerful speakers, delivering  practical, action-based strategies investors can implement immediately, including: Team building and legal structure for sustainable growth Underwriting and deal structuring across asset types Capital stack and financing strategies (hard money, DSCR, BRRRR) Construction and project management systems that protect margins Renovations and design decisions that directly impact ROI Scaling operations with stronger vendor management and streamlined processes This experience is designed for investors with three or more completed deals who are actively executing BRRRRs, flips, or small-scale development projects and looking to build repeatable, scalable systems. With Seattle’s competitive housing market and continued investor activity, the conference offers timely insight into how local operators are navigating tighter margins, rising costs, and evolving financing strategies. In addition to the conference, on March 29, a limited Premium Property Tour will take attendees inside active Seattle investment properties, where James and the speakers will walk through real deals and answer questions in small groups.

item.title: Connect for Good: Sammamish Nonprofit Expo
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: SPC/Sammamish Presbyterian Church, 22522 NE Inglewood Hill Rd, Sammamish, WA

Connect for Good: Sammamish Nonprofit Expo 2026 Sponsored by: Puget Sound Energy, Sammamish Friends and Lead-ology Mark your calendar and join us for Connect for Good, a community-wide nonprofit expo designed to bring people, purpose, and impact together. On Saturday, March 28, from 10:00am–2:00pm, the Sammamish community is invited to gather at Sammamish Presbyterian Church for an inspiring afternoon of connection and service. Explore 30\+ local nonprofits, discover meaningful volunteer opportunities, and learn how you—and your family—can make a difference close to home. The event will feature youth leadership and student involvement opportunities, along with a Youth Zone & activities, making it welcoming and engaging for all ages. Whether you’re a student, family, community member, or volunteer, Connect for Good is the perfect place to support causes you care about, get involved in service, and strengthen our local community—all in one place. Hosted by the Sammamish Chamber of Commerce, in collaboration with local nonprofits and community partners.Nonprofit participation and sponsorship opportunities available.

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

item.title: Free Tax Help / Ayuda gratuita con los impuestos
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 11 AM

Sunday, March 29, 2026, 11am - 5pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Spanish support may be provided per request. ---------------------------------------------------------------- Voluntarios capacitados estarán disponibles para responder preguntas y preparar declaraciones de impuestos personales. Se puede proporcionar ayuda en español a pedido. Location Link: South Park Branch Room Location: Meeting Room / Sala de reuniones Registration Required: Registration is not required / No se requiere inscripción. Detailed Description: This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County ., ----------------------------------------------------------------Este servicio es gratuito y solo está disponible para personas individuales. El servicio gratuito no está disponible para declaraciones de impuestos de empresas., Para recibir ayuda fiscal, traiga los siguientes documentos:, Documentos necesarios:, Tarjetas del Seguro Social o Números de Identificación Fiscal Individual \(ITIN\) de todas las personas que figuran en la declaración de impuestos, Prueba de ingresos, por ejemplo, formularios W2 y 1099 \(prestaciones del Seguro Social\), Documentos oficiales con membrete de la Administración del Seguro Social, Tarjeta de Medicare \(si el número de Seguro Social va seguido de una "A"\), Esté preparado para proporcionar información sobre la cobertura sanitaria de cada persona que figura en la declaración., Se recomienda:, La declaración de impuestos del año pasado, si está disponible., Números de cuenta bancaria y de ruta para el depósito directo de los reembolsos., Se aceptarán fotocopias o impresiones de computadora de cualquiera de estos documentos., Debido a recortes presupuestarios federales, el IRS no puede proporcionar a la biblioteca ciertos formularios fiscales y folletos de instrucciones., La mayoría de los formularios del IRS están disponibles en línea en www.irs.gov ., Puede imprimir los formularios en blanco y negro de forma gratuita desde las computadoras de la biblioteca: www.spl.org/Imprimir , Gracias a nuestro socio United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700., ----------------------------------------------------------------Los centros de ayuda fiscal de United Way atienden a cualquier persona que haya ganado menos de 80,000 dólares en 2025 \(o menos de 96,000 dólares para contribuyentes que no sean solteros\)., Para obtener más información sobre los requisitos, comuníquese con United Way al 206-461-3700 Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636., ----------------------------------------------------------------Si tiene alguna pregunta sobre este evento, pregúntenos o llame al 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Póngase en contacto con LEAP al menos siete días antes del evento para solicitar las adaptaciones., Todos los programas de la biblioteca son gratuitos y abiertos al público.

Event details

item.title: Natasha Zimmers with Naja Lund Aparico - 'Tiny Hiker' and 'Seasons by the Lake: Adventures in Greenl
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Third Place Books Lake Forest Park, 17171 Bothell Way NE,, Lake Forest Park, WA

Welcome spring by celebrating the joy of exploring the great outdoors! Third Place Books welcomes authors Natasha Zimmers and Naja Lund Aparico to our Lake Forest Park store for a storytime featuring Natasha's book Tiny Hikers and Naja's book Seasons by the Lake: Adventures in Greenland. This event is free and open to the public. RSVP is not required. Readers of all ages welcome! About Tiny Hikers . . . "Offers a realistic, joyful depiction of a young adventurer and reflects the author's experience as an outdoor educator. A standout debut that will inspire young readers to explore the outdoors." -- School Library Journal \(SLJ\), starred review The tiny hiker finishes loading up her pack and excitedly gets on the trail, leading her family through the ancient trees toward their beach campsite. At first it's fun to explore--she finds a hollow log, wriggles under a fallen tree, and spots a slimy slug But then the trail starts getting steeper, and when her legs get tired, she trips on a root and takes a hard fall. Now her knee hurts, her elbows hurt...everything hurts She just wants to give up. But after a little break, a tasty snack and an exciting glimpse of the beach, the tiny hiker feels better and decides she will make it. With one final scamper over some giant beach logs, she finds the beach. Time to play And at the end of the long day, after sitting by the campfire, the tiny hiker gets cozy in her sleeping bag and falls fast asleep. About Seasons by the Lake. . . Spend a year experiencing the seasons in Greenland with two Inuit siblings. Brothers Minik and Nuka have a whole year of adventures ahead in Greenland, including finding ice shards from glaciers in the spring to picking crowberries during the summer when the sun barely sets. The darkness of winter reveals Arsarnerit, or the Aurora Borealis, above as the two whisper about the Inuit myth behind the lights. Greenlandic Inuk author Naja Lund Aparico combines a sweet sibling story with an exploration of the seasons in the Arctic featuring stunning illustrations by German and Choctaw illustrator Alex Nees that captures the sweeping landscape. Natasha Zimmers loves being outside in all seasons and weather, but her favourite place to be is on a wild west coast beach, backpacking with her own not-so-tiny hikers. She teaches outdoor education and loves seeing the world through the eyes of her students as they discover the wonders of nature. Naja Lund Aparico is a Greenlandic Inuk author with a background in international relations and currently a second year MFA graduate student at the Institute of American Indian Arts \(IAIA\) with a focus on creative non-fiction. Having grown up in Greenland, she now lives in the Pacific Northwest with her family. Aparico’s children’s books and essays are centered on the indigenous identity with an emphasis on kin, heritage, and the environment. Her US debut picture book, SEASONS BY THE LAKE, will be published by Dial / PRH in March of 2026, while her second picture book \(untitled\) is set to be published by Candlewick in late Spring of 2027, and her third picture book will be published by Dial/PRH in late 2027. Want a signed edition of the featured book, but can't make it to the event? Order through our website or over the phone, and write your request for a signature or personalization in the comments field at checkout. Please call the hosting store if you're placing your order within 24 hours of the event. For media or access inquiries, please email events@thirdplacebooks.com or call our Lake Forest Park store at \(206\) 366-3311.

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 11 AM

Monday, March 30, 2026, 11am - 5pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 5 - Mixing Chamber Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Drop-in tax help is offered on a first-come, first-served basis., This service is free and available to individuals only., The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required Documents: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

item.title: Virtual Workshop - Become a Small Business Owner Through Franchising
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Monday, March 30, 2026, 1 - 3pm If you are considering franchising as a career option, or actively working towards a franchise agreement, this program is for you. Location Link: Online Registration Required: Registration is required. Detailed Description: This workshop is designed both for individuals at the early stage of considering franchising as a career option and those actively considering a franchise but who have not yet signed the agreement., Topics covered include: Understand what is and is not a franchise, Define a positive relationship with the franchisor, Laws and legal requirements that apply to franchising in Washington State, Important criteria in choosing a franchise, what questions to ask and how to ask them, Research hints and tools, A view into a method to segment franchises in six different categories, A discussion on financing, incorporation and preparation relating to a franchise award, Navigating the myriad of franchise opportunities available, Doing your due diligence, Narrowing down your search, What to consider and what matters to you, Answers to your specific questions, About the Presenter:, Jeff Levy is an educator, author and coach. Herelocated from NYC for a 3-person tech startup up in WA State, has managed up to 300 people across international borders and has been a franchisee since 2002. His personal experiences include raising capital, starting and acquiring businesses and investing in a franchise during a career transition. He has also served on multiple boards, committees and has contributed voluntary work on community projects., Registration is required. Register here . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Matchmaking Your Business Build your network. Save time. Strengthen your resources.
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Mercer Island Community Center, 2247 84th Ave SE, Mercer Island, WA

Matchmaking Your Business is a structured, high-energy networking event designed to connect Realtors with trusted industry vendors. Location: Mercer Island Community Center 8236 SE 24th Street, Mercer Island, WA 98040 Parking: Free When: Tuesday, March 31st, 2026﻿ ﻿Time: 11am to 1pm Matchmaking Your Business is a structured, high-energy networking event designed to connect Realtors with trusted industry vendors. The goal is simple: Help Realtors build reliable vendor partnerships that elevate the client experience, streamline transactions, and support long-term business growth. Tired of calling vendor after vendor trying to find someone reliable for your clients? Come meet them in one place. Our WCR Seattle Metro Vendor Speed Networking Event gives you the chance to connect one-on-one with trusted industry professionals who can help support your business and your clients. Build your network. Save time. Strengthen your resources. Just real connections that build your book of business. Come ready to connect, collaborate, and fine the right matches for your business.

Detailed Description: Discuss relevant topics in small groups to gain self-confidence and improve your speaking and listening. This class is for adult English language learners levels 2 and up \(beginner/intermediate\)., The current series run: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, Thanks to our partner Literacy Source ., -----------------------------------------------------------------, Practique su inglés. Hable sobre temas relevantes en grupos pequeños para ganar confianza en sí mismo y mejorar su habla y comprensión auditiva. Esta clase está dirigida a estudiantes adultos de inglés de nivel 2 en adelante \(principiante/intermedio\)., Las clases actuales se imparten del: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Luyện tập tiếng Anh của quý vị. Thảo luận các chủ đề liên quan trong các nhóm nhỏ để có được sự tự tin và cải thiện khả năng nói và nghe của quý vị. Знайдіть безкоштовні розмовні групи, щоб практикувати свої навички англійської мови разом з іншими дорослими учнями. Обговорюйте актуальні теми в невеликих групах, щоб стати впевненішими в собі та вдосконалити свої навички говоріння й сприйняття мови на слух. Цей клас призначений для дорослих, які вивчають англійську мову на рівні 2 і вище \(початковий/середній\)., Поточні заняття проводяться: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Найдите бесплатные разговорные группы, чтобы прорабатывать свои навыки владения английским с другими взрослыми учащимися. Обсуждайте актуальные темы в малых группах, чтобы обрести уверенность в своих силах и развить умение говорить и воспринимать язык на слух., Текущие занятия проходят: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Pratiquez votre anglais. item.title: AI Essentials for Jobs: Boosting Your Job Search Strategy
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 1:30 - 3pm Discover how to effectively use Artificial Intelligence \(AI\) to enhance your job search and all aspects of your application and interview preparation. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 4 - Room 2 Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Join Employment Specialist Corey Minatani from Evergreen Goodwill&'s Job Training and Education Center \(JTEC\) for an introductory workshop designed to help you leverage artificial intelligence to enhance your job search., Learn how AI can streamline resume building, job matching, interview preparation, and networking, making your job hunt smarter and more efficient. Whether you're new to AI or looking to sharpen your digital tools, this session will provide practical strategies to boost your chances of success in today&'s competitive job market., Thanks to our partner Evergreen Goodwill Job Training & Education. item.title: Power Connect: Bombo
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: Bombo Italian Kitchen, 1600 9th Ave, Seattle, WA

Description Power Connect at Bombo Italian Date: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 Time: 4:30 PM Location: Bombo Italian Presented by: U.S. Bank Tickets: $25 for members and $35 for guests Join GSBA for a new Power Connect at Bombo Italian on Tuesday, March 31 at 4:30 PM. Power Connect is our signature networking series-an easygoing space to meet fellow LGBTQ\+ and allied professionals, reconnect with familiar faces, and build relationships that support your business and our community. Come as you are, bring a colleague, and expect the standard Power Connect vibes: warm welcomes, real conversation, and connections that keep going after the event. What to Expect A welcoming, relationship-first environment Space for casual conversation and meaningful introductions A great chance to meet GSBA members, partners, and friends An easy way to plug into the GSBA community Accessibility If you have access needs or accommodation requests, please reach out to GSBA in advance and we'll do our best to support you: membership@thegsb.org Thank You to Our Presenting Sponsor Power Connect: Bombo is presented by U.S. Bank, supporting LGBTQ\+ and allied businesses and helping strengthen connections across our region.

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Wednesday, April 1, 2026, 10am - 1:30pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Broadview Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns. To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return., Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return., Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents willbe accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov ., You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner AARP . Event Logistics: AARP Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025., For eligibility details, contact AARP at 888-227-7669. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session\\\*\*\* Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate \(paper, t-shirts, etc\) - Water based ink Vera \*does not\* allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. \*\*FAQ\*\* 1. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Norwegian Language Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

This nine-week series will allow you to jump into the basics of Norwegian language and culture in a supportive, face-to-face classroom environment. Students will learn essential vocabulary, everyday phrases, pronunciation, and simple grammar structures through interactive activities, conversation practice, and cultural activities. No prior foreign language experience is required; ages 16+. Every Thursday, April 2 - May 28 NOR1A: 5-6:30pm - NOR1A is for absolute beginners with no prior language experience NOR1C: 6:45-8pm - NOR1C is for students who may know some basic Norwegian and want to grow their skills

item.title: LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Lake Washington Institute of Technology - LWTech, 11605 132nd Ave NE, Kirkland, WA

LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP Friday, April 3, 2026 Lake Washington Institute of Technology – 11605 132nd Avenue NE, Kirkland, WA 98034-8506 TITLE SPONSOR - DURANTA The Washington Association of Landscape Professionals is excited to offer the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP training in partnership with the National Association of Landscape Professionals. WALP will be offering the LANDSCAPE TECHNICIAN BOOTCAMP in APRIL 3, 2026 at Lake Washington Insitute of Technology that will be comprehensive training for your staff and workforce. Hands-On Training For Your Crews! The new Landscape Technician Boot Camp will provide hands-on training for your crews. Based on the curriculum for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam, this industry-vetted training will help prepare your crews to work more effectively in 2026. Boot Camp includes training in techniques and safety in 15 different competency areas organized into 3 disciplines presented in Spanish and English! Bonus! This course also helps prepare people for the Landscape Industry Certified Technician Exterior exam. So, once you’ve trained your team, consider registering them to become certified through the National Association of Landscape Professionals \(NALP\). Registration Costs: WALP Members \(First 5 registrants\): $95.00 per person WALP Members \(6\+ registrants\): $80.00 per person NON-WALP Members : $145.00 per person

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Homebuyer Education Workshop
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: ULMS, 105 14th Avenue, Suite 200, Seattle, WA

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. Once this form has been submitted, be sure to check your email for a digital confirmation, as well as additional follow up and important information from a ULMS Housing Department staff member.   In-person workshops are held every 1st and 2nd Saturday of the month from 10:00AM – 3:30PM. They will be held at our Main Office at the following address: 105 14th Ave, Suite 200 Seattle, WA 98122.

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Sailing Shore School
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: CWB Boathouse, Lake Union, Seattle, WA

This 2-hour interactive course will introduce you to the basics of sailing. Topics will include terminology for parts of the boat and points of sail, as well as the basic theory of sailing. With the class you will receive the CWB Sailing Primer! Shore School can be taken independently or as part of the SailNOW! or US Sailing Basic Keelboat Certification courses. More information here

item.title: Spring Educator Workshop: Seeing Self and Place in the Modern Northwest
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Explore the symbolic and deeply personal visions of artists who helped define a unique modernist movement in the Pacific Northwest. Centering Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest, educators will gain insight on how artists blended abstraction and nature to express complex ideas about identity, place, and purpose. Participants will engage in a studio art activity inspired by the exhibition, explore student-led inquiry models, and reflect and connect with fellow educators. Tickets This workshop is free to attend, but reservations are required. This workshop includes resources and Washington State Clock Hours. K-12 educators, paraeducators, subject area coaches, and administrators are welcome to attend. Support for K-12 programs during the 2025-2026 school year is provided by the Foster Foundation. Partial, sustained support for SAM's K-12 programs is provided by an endowment established in 1999 by a National Endowment for the Humanities challenge grant and the generous contributions of matching donors, including the Ann P. Wyckoff Education Endowment. The William Randolph Hearst Endowed Fund for Education Programs at the Seattle Art Museum has supported SAM K-12 programs since 1994.

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

item.title: Spy Masters Academy
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Class Experience Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the ticket link to book the camp!

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Capitol Hill & First Hill Business Safety Summit
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description You are invited to join fellow Capitol Hill and First Hill businesses for a Safety Summit at the Silver Cloud Hotel \(1100 Broadway\) on Mon, Apr 6 from 3:00 to 5:00 p.m. This gathering is designed specifically for businesses operating at the street level. This is a great opportunity to meet the new East Precinct Captain, hear about helpful programs from the City, and connect with neighboring businesses facing many of the same challenges. Our goal is to work together to support and strengthen what makes the business districts of Capitol Hill and First Hill so unique and special. This event is brought to you by the 15th Avenue Business Improvement Area, Broadway Business Improvement Area, First Hill Improvement Association, and GSBA: Capitol Hill, with additional support from 4N & Associates and Silver Cloud Hotel. Meet Our New East Precinct Captain Captain James Britt, SPD Community Assisted Response & Engagement \(CARE\) Chief Amy Barden, CARE Learn About Community Assisted Response & Engagement \(CARE\) Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design \(CPTED\) Back to Business Program: Storefront Repair Fund and Storefront Security Fund Share Bring your safety challenges, concerns and questions Program 3pm Doors open 3:30pm Speaking program starts followed by social Please RSVP Space is limited. Free Parking! Light food provided. Please bring cash for no host bar. Pre-event Survey Help shape the conversation at the Safety Summit by sharing any safety challenges, concerns, or questions you would like addressed. Please submit responses by Friday, April 3 so we can share common themes with presenters in advance. If you cannot attend, we still welcome your input and will share an event recap afterward.

item.title: Building a More Welcoming Washington: Seattle's FIFA World Cup Inclusion Training
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: GSBA- Washington State&'s LGBTQ+ Chamber of Commerce, 400 E Pine St Ste 322, Seattle, WA

Description Inclusion Training Initiative: Building a More Welcoming Washington As Washington prepares to welcome the world for FIFA World Cup 2026, local businesses will be on the front lines of hospitality-serving visitors, supporting staff, and representing the values of our region every day. GSBA is partnering with SeattleFWC26 to offer Inclusion Training workshops designed to help small businesses and organizations feel prepared, confident, and welcoming. These sessions provide practical, real-world guidance on allyship, gender inclusion, and LGBTQ\+ cultural competency, with a focus on everyday customer and workplace interactions. About the Inclusion Training Initiative As part of SeattleFWC26's commitment to ensuring every fan, visitor, and resident feels welcome, the Inclusion Training Initiative-delivered in partnership with GSBA-equips local businesses and organizations with tools to create authentically inclusive environments for LGBTQ\+ customers, employees, and communities. About River Teslar River \(she/her\) is a trans woman and nonprofit professional with four years of experience at GSBA developing and facilitating LGBTQ\+ Inclusion Trainings through a social justice- and trauma-informed lens. Drawing from lived experience and community-based work, she creates learning spaces grounded in empathy, curiosity, and practical application, where participants feel supported engaging at any level. Key Activities ⚽ Four Inclusion Workshops: Hosted by GSBA and tailored for small businesses, nonprofits, and frontline teams. ⚽ Interactive Learning: Sessions focus on allyship, gender inclusion, and LGBTQ\+ cultural competency in the workplace, with real-world scenarios and discussion. ⚽ Accessible Participation: Two in-person workshops hosted at GSBA's Capitol Hill office \(or other community venues\) and two virtual workshops open to participants statewide. ⚽ Practical Tools: Participants receive actionable guidance and resources to help strengthen inclusive policies, communications, and customer experiences. Outcomes & Benefits ⚽ More Inclusive Businesses: Participating organizations gain the knowledge to create spaces that welcome all genders, identities, and relationships. ⚽ Community Visibility: Strengthens Washington's reputation as a leader in inclusive tourism and community hospitality ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026. ⚽ Economic Impact: Inclusive environments encourage repeat visitation, word-of-mouth referrals, and stronger customer loyalty. ⚽ Sustained Legacy: Builds long-term capacity for inclusion that extends beyond the tournament, leaving a lasting mark on Washington's business and tourism ecosystem. Workshop Schedule Virtual Workshops ⚽ Thursday, January 15 - 10:00 AM ⚽ Wednesday, March 4 - 3:00 PM In-Person Workshops ⚽ Monday, February 9 - 10:00 AM ⚽ Monday, April 6 - 3:00 PM Learn more about the initiative: SeattleFWC26 Inclusion Training Initiative

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Digital Humanities: Intersections and Challenges
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Advancement & Alumni Building, Stuart T. Rolfe Room, Seattle, WA

What Is Digital Humanities—and Why Does It Matter Now? Digital humanities is an interdisciplinary field that combines humanistic inquiry with digital tools to transform how we study culture, language, history, and society. From digital archives and multilingual publishing to data-driven cultural analysis, the field is reshaping research, teaching, and public scholarship worldwide. The Roundglass India Center & Technology Ethics Initiative invite students and faculty to explore this rapidly evolving space through a special lecture by Professor Nirmala Menon, an internationally recognized leader in digital humanities. Professor Menon is Chair Professor at the School of Humanities and Social Sciences at the Indian Institute of Technology Indore, where she leads the Digital Humanities and Publishing Research Group and is Chair of the JPN National Centre of Excellence in the Humanities. She also serves as an Affiliate Research Professor at the University of Oxford and is currently a Fulbright Fellow at Northeastern University \(2025–26\). Her scholarship spans postcolonial studies, digital humanities, and multilingual knowledge systems, with a sustained focus on decolonizing global knowledge infrastructures. Dr. Menon's Talk: Digital Knowledge Infrastructures and Digital Humanities Projects: Intersections and Challenges In this timely talk, Professor Menon examines the uneven and evolving trajectory of digital humanities \(DH\) in India and the broader research ecosystem. She explores the skepticism and resistance the field sometimes encounters—including concerns about distancing from core humanities questions or borrowing the rhetoric of STEM for legitimacy—while making the case that DH is far more than its critiques. Professor Menon argues that humanities scholars must actively harness, interrogate, and shape digital technologies to ensure that enduring humanistic questions remain central to technological development and deployment. Drawing on major initiatives—including Project KSHIP at IIT Indore, the DST-sponsored SINDHU project, the Electronic Literature from India anthology, and the work of Open Access India—she highlights efforts to decolonize knowledge systems, expand multilingual scholarship, and make research more accessible and equitable. The talk will also address the continuing structural challenges facing humanities research infrastructures and invite conversation about the future of digital scholarship in global and Indian contexts. Students, faculty, and researchers interested in digital scholarship, global humanities, publishing, technology and society, and South Asia are especially encouraged to attend.

item.title: Type Design Basics
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview So you want to design a typeface? Learn how to hand-draw letters and digitize them to create your own unique functional font! In this practical workshop, students will create an original text typeface based on traditional European calligraphy. Whether you want to develop your typography skills, improve your lettering practice, or become a type designer, learning the fundamentals of type design can bring your skills to the next level. Explore both analog media \(pen on paper\) and digital media \(computer\). Learn how to use a calligraphy pen to develop an understanding of the underlying structure of the Latin alphabet. Then you can work on improving your drawings digitally, and create a typeface using Glyphs \(or the font editor of your choice\).   Class time is dedicated to working on your project with individualized feedback, supplemented by lectures, technical demonstrations, and group critique. By the end of the class, you will have a working text typeface of your own creation, as well as a sketch-to-screen workflow that you can use for future font creation. Students will be encouraged to spend up to 10 hours on homework outside of class time. Designing a typeface is a laborious process, but time flies when experimenting, drawing, and editing! This class is offered in partnership with the amazing Seattle Creative Academy \(but this class is not part of their program and is not for credit/degree\)! See also: Experimental Lettering and other Graphic Design or art classes on our website. Learning Objectives Learn the basics of calligraphy Understand the structure and patterning of the Latin alphabet Create your own original letterforms Digitize and refine your original design Learn how to optimize a typeface for text Become familiar with a sketch-to-screen digital type design workflow Who Might Enjoy This Class? Anyone who wants to develop their own custom font Graphic designers, illustrators, lettering artists, type designers -- anyone with related experience who would like to hone their type design skills Architects, engineers, artists, crafters, writers, book lovers -- anyone interested in the form of letters who would enjoy creating and using their own handmade font No prerequisites, but basic graphic design/typography knowledge is recommended

item.title: Artist's Way Classes Winter 2026 are Now Open for Registration
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: The Fauntleroy Schoolhouse, 9131 California Avenue SW, Seattle, WA

The Artist's Way Classes for Winter 2026 via Zoom or In-Person are Now Open for Registration The Artist's Way by Julia Cameron is an international bestseller on the subject of creativity. Whether you see yourself as an artist or not, this book and class can be an incredibly useful resource to tap into your creative side which can benefit many parts of your life. Led by Creativity Instructor, Kate Gavigan, the 2 hour a week 12 week long class will take students through the 12 chapters with an emphasis on the accompanying chapter exercises and additional creativity exercises and strategies \(i.e. meditation tools\). This material can work with anyone, no matter how dormant or energized their creative life may be.  Students report that as a result of going through our Artist's Way class they more fully and genuinely know themselves, they discover or re-discover their gifts and talents and have more fun and joy in their lives. To quote Julia, "In order to retrieve your creativity, you need to find it." The basic tools and the additional creativity exercises that we incorporate into the 12 week course can help you do just that. No prerequisites required for this class.  Students also meet with the instructor for a one on one chat on Zoom for 30 minutes prior to the first class and before the last class. Section I: January 19 – April 6 – via Zoom Monday Mornings, 10:00 am – noon $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section II:  January 19 – April 6 - via Zoom Monday Evenings, 6:30 – 8:30 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section III:  – January 21 – April 8 via Zoom Wednesdays, 3:00 – 5:00 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section IV: January 21 – April 8 via In-PERSON Wednesdays, 6:30 – 8:30 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) This In-Person Class held in West Seattle at the Fauntleroy Schoolhouse, 9131 California Ave. SW, Seattle, WA Instructor for all sections: Kate Gavigan Note: Students will use the book The Artist’s Way by Julia Cameron. Students are responsible for purchasing their own copy of The Artist’s Way by Julia Cameron. To Register or for more information: https://seattleartistswaycenter.com/class-offerings For testimonials from past students: https://seattleartistswaycenter.com/class-testimonials Kate Gavigan is passionate about The Artist's Way after offering classes since 2009 and seeing the positive changes in her students' lives and having personally experienced the benefits of the materials. Kate worked in social services as a trainer for over 10 years when she found herself drawn to the material in The Artist's Way, which helped her uncover a passion for theatre and the arts. She credits her mid-life career change \(now working at her dream jobs as an Artist's Way Instructor and Speaker since 2009, a Creativity Coach, a Home Organizer and pursuing her interest in Acting and Music\) to having gone through The Artist's Way.

item.title: Livestream- Retiring from the UWRP: An Overview
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

Online Thursday, Apr 9, 2026, 9 a.m. - 12 p.m. PDT Who should attend: Faculty, librarians and professional staff who are participating in the UW Retirement Plan \(UWRP\) and expect to retire within the next 12 months. This 3 hour workshop is designed for those retiring within the year and covers the following topics: Retirement eligibility Completing the UW retirement application Retiree medical plan choices and enrollment Applying for Medicare Leave balances and VEBA, if applicable Notifying your department about your separation Working after retirement Accessibility Contact: benefits@uw.edu Event Types: Lectures/Seminars Event sponsors: UW Human Resources Benefits office More info: ucs.admin.uw.edu…

item.description:

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 12 PM

First Thursdays in the Native Plant Nursery! Calling all n ative plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us every first Th ursday of each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nu rsery. Every year we host a couple thousand native plants in the nursery u ntil they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great opportunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted pla nts, up-potting or re-potting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, a nd watering. When: Thursdays, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants , boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/B4AFB

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

Event details

item.title: Contemporary Dance for Every Body
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Would you like to cultivate body awareness through movement? Explore a grounded and natural approach to moving your body, led by a contemporary dance instructor. Through floorwork, movement sequences, and phrasing, this class cultivates body awareness. Explore efficiency in movement transitions. Develop a deeper understanding of how your body moves naturally in relation to the floor, emphasizing organic flow, continuity, spirality, softness, core engagement, and the intelligent use of gravity. Open your mind and body through guided movement! Explore points of support, releasing tension, and engaging the core to facilitate flow and prevent injury. Movement principles will draw from contemporary dance and somatic practices, encouraging dancers to move with clarity, sensitivity, and presence. This class is for dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners, and anyone interested in exploring movement in their bodies -- no experience necessary. Designed to be inclusive, this class offers both accessibility and challenge to movers at various levels and all backgrounds.  Learning Objectives Understand and embody key principles of movement floorwork, including gravity, spirality, and core initiation. Improve movement continuity and flow between levels \(floor to standing and vice versa\). Explore softness and efficiency through core engagement. Identify and use anatomical points of support for safe and fluid movement. Develop new dance tools Feel more connected with the possibilities of your body in a conscious way to live a more present, healthy, and aware life. Who Might Enjoy This Class? Adult learners of all backgrounds \(18\+\), including dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners Anyone interested in embodied movement practices. Some prior movement or dance experience is helpful but not required. The class will be structured to accommodate varying levels of experience and physical ability.

item.title: Norwegian Language Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

This nine-week series will allow you to jump into the basics of Norwegian language and culture in a supportive, face-to-face classroom environment. Students will learn essential vocabulary, everyday phrases, pronunciation, and simple grammar structures through interactive activities, conversation practice, and cultural activities. No prior foreign language experience is required; ages 16+. Every Thursday, April 2 - May 28 NOR1A: 5-6:30pm - NOR1A is for absolute beginners with no prior language experience NOR1C: 6:45-8pm - NOR1C is for students who may know some basic Norwegian and want to grow their skills
